changes: OpenClaw did not cut a new stable tag in this 2026-04-27 scan, but it pushed three fresh prereleases on 2026-04-26: v2026.4.25-beta.2, beta.3, and beta.4.
Hermes Agent still has no newer release than v2026.4.23, but it climbed again to roughly 119.1k stars in this scan.
AstrBot shipped stable v4.23.6 on 2026-04-27 05:09 UTC.
PicoClaw posted another nightly build on 2026-04-27 01:06 UTC, while HiClaw and other orchestration-layer names kept shipping and updating without changing the broader category map.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw's multiple same-day betas still signal extremely fast iteration, even on a day without a fresh stable anchor.
Hermes' continued growth reinforces that the learning-loop lane is compounding audience attention faster than most derivative rewrites.
AstrBot's release matters as adjacent benchmark pressure, because the broader messaging-agent market is also shipping quickly, not just direct OpenClaw descendants.
The rest of today's movement looks like cadence confirmation, not a thesis break: the ecosystem is active, but the durable strategic read still holds.
